document_summary: Profile of the Communication Association of China (CAC), the national academic organization for communication studies that co-organized the 11th China Communication Conference where Dehuan Liu won the Excellent Paper Award. Describes the organization's national standing, government approval, membership composition, business scope, and organizational charter. Serves as source credibility evidence for the award.

snippets.text: "The Communication Association of China" is a national academic organization for communication studies, approved by the Chinese Academy of Social Sciences and registered with the Ministry of Civil Affairs of the People's Republic of China. The full name of the association is "The Communication Branch of the China News Culture Promotion Association," also known as the "Communication Association of China."
snippets.subject: Communication Association of China
snippets.reasoning: Core description establishing the CAC as a nationally recognized academic organization, approved by CASS and registered with the Ministry of Civil Affairs. Demonstrates the legitimacy and prestige of the body that awarded Liu's paper prize.

snippets.text: This society is based on the principle of voluntariness. It is made up of academic units such as journalism and communication departments and institutions from universities and colleges across the country, journalism and communication research organizations, and news media. It has faculty from university journalism and communication departments, journalists, etc., as individual members. The current president of the China News Culture Promotion Association is Dongdong Li, the Deputy Director of the National Press and Publication Administration. Professor Fan Jingyi serves as an advisor. Yungong Yin, the director of the Institute of Journalism and Communication at the Chinese Academy of Social Sciences, is the honorary president. The first president was researcher Anxiang Ming from the Institute of Journalism and Communication at the Chinese Academy of Social Sciences. The organization is under the supervision of the Institute of Journalism and Communication at the Chinese Academy of Social Sciences.
snippets.subject: Communication Association of China
snippets.reasoning: Details the membership composition (national universities, research organizations, news media) and leadership (Deputy Director of National Press and Publication Administration, CASS director), establishing the organization's national scope and high-level affiliations.

snippets.text: This organization, based on the principle of voluntariness, is an academic group composed of journalism and communication colleges and departments of national universities, news and communication research institutions, and news media as unit members. It includes faculty from journalism and communication colleges and departments, researchers from news and communication research institutions, and journalists as individual members.
snippets.subject: Communication Association of China
snippets.reasoning: Charter-level description of the CAC's nature as a voluntary national academic group comprising universities, research institutions, and media organizations nationwide.

snippets.text: This organization abides by the constitution, laws, regulations, and national policies, adheres to social ethics, unites scholars in communication studies nationwide, as well as all groups and individuals interested in communication studies. It is committed to addressing theoretical and practical issues arising in social practice of communication studies, promoting China's modernization, and the healthy development of global communication exchanges.
snippets.subject: Communication Association of China
snippets.reasoning: States the organization's mission to unite communication scholars nationwide and promote global communication exchanges, reinforcing its role as the premier national body in communication studies.

snippets.text: Article 7: The "Communication Association of China" adopts the principle of voluntary membership. Members are divided into institutional members and individual members. Institutional members are the communication faculties or departments of national universities, news research institutions, and news organizations; individual members are teachers of communication faculties or departments of universities, scholars from news research institutions, professionals in journalism and media, and all individuals interested in communication studies who meet the necessary conditions.
snippets.subject: Communication Association of China
snippets.reasoning: Defines the membership criteria and structure of the CAC, showing it comprises national university communication faculties, research institutions, and news organizations, reinforcing the breadth and selectivity of the organization.
